title: Binary Tree Height (recursive)
description: Given a binary tree encoded as parent array (parent[i] = parent of node i, -1 for root), compute its height: the number of edges on the longest root-to-leaf path.
inputSpec: First line: n. Second line: n integers, the parent of each node (0-indexed, -1 = root).
outputSpec: The height of the tree (0 for a single node).

botCodes.code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(200000)
n=int(input())
p=list(map(int,input().split()))
ch=[[] for _ in range(n)]
root=0
for i,par in enumerate(p):
    if par==-1:root=i
    else:ch[par].append(i)
def h(u):return 0 if not ch[u] else 1+max(h(c) for c in ch[u])
print(h(root))
